title: The Weekly Challenge - 241
task1_desc: You are given an array (3 or more members) of integers in increasing order and a positive integer.
task1_body: You are given an array (3 or more members) of integers in increasing order and a positive integer. Write a script to find out the number of unique `Arithmetic Triplets` satisfying the following rules: a) i < j < k b) nums[j] - nums[i] == diff c) nums[k] - nums[j] == diff #### Example 1 Input: @nums = (0, 1, 4, 6, 7, 10) $diff = 3 Output: 2 Index (1, 2, 4) is an arithmetic triplet because both 7 - 4 == 3 and 4 - 1 == 3. Index (2, 4, 5) is an arithmetic triplet because both 10 - 7 == 3 and 7 - 4 == 3. #### Example 2 Input: @nums = (4, 5, 6, 7, 8, 9) $diff = 2 Output: 2 (0, 2, 4) is an arithmetic triplet because both 8 - 6 == 2 and 6 - 4 == 2. (1, 3, 5) is an arithmetic triplet because both 9 - 7 == 2 and 7 - 5 == 2.
task2_desc: You are given an array of unique positive integers greater than 2.
task2_body:
You are given an array of unique positive integers greater than 2. Write a script to sort them in ascending order of the count of their prime factors, tie-breaking by ascending value. #### Example 1 Input: @int = (11, 8, 27, 4) Output: (11, 4, 8, 27)) Prime factors of 11 => 11 Prime factors of 4 => 2, 2 Prime factors of 8 => 2, 2, 2 Prime factors of 27 => 3, 3, 3
Last date to submit the solution **23:59 (UK Time) Sunday 5th November 2023**.
